meanings.definition: not to be placated or appeased or moved by entreaty
examples: Russia's final hour, it seemed, approached with inexorable certainty

meanings.definition: impervious to pleas, persuasion, requests, reason; ; - W.Churchill
examples: he is adamant in his refusal to change his mind
Cynthia was inexorable; she would have none of him
an intransigent conservative opposed to every liberal tendency
